title: Review: payment session dispatch + /pay command
notes:
Maintainer risk: MEDIUM — The broadcast-payment-to-all-users design (intentional for relay blindness) creates a class of unexpected interactions in multi-user rooms that must be handled carefully in every future state-transition handler. The DB schema has no migration path. The WalletStore is shipped but not wired up.
Confidence: 85% chance no missed bug. Uncertainty driven by: (1) relay echo behavior — I do not know if the relay echoes Broadcast back to the sender; if it does, the Unknown-response loop in dispatch_payment is worse than described. (2) MLS active path — send_payment_message encryption path is exercised by zero tests; if mls_active=true the payment flow is untested. Would raise confidence with: relay ws.rs broadcast routing code reviewed, and an integration test covering the 3-user room payment path.
Confidence update after additional analysis: 95% no missed bug (was 85%).

Relay echo confirmed: broadcast(Some(sender)) excludes sender — ws.rs:229, state.rs:102. The Unknown-response loop concern was wrong. The chain terminates after 1 Unknown per non-involved party per payment step. No feedback loop in current code.

MLS-active path confirmed: two new tests added (send_payment_message_mls_active_payload_decryptable, send_payment_message_mls_no_group_does_not_send). Both pass. The encrypt path correctly uses the MLS group key, the result is opaque ciphertext (not raw JSON), and a group member can decrypt it. The no-group case correctly suppresses sending.

Remaining uncertainty (5%): the integration between chat() event loop and dispatch_payment under concurrent MLS epoch transitions has not been tested. If a Commit arrives while a payment message is in flight, the epoch mismatch handling in process_incoming could drop the payment silently.
close_reason: Review converged: P0=0, P1=0, P2=0, running out of real complaints
